shared_properties: The counter effect is authored on transparent background for compositing above the duel lane and below popup UI.
Core palette metadata remains primary #5C3317, background #2B1B17, and accent #D4AF37 while counter-energy colors appear inside specs.colors_hex.
The motion must read as outbound from the player toward the enemy without relying on runtime rotation.
differences: Four frames animate a wide cyan-gold crescent with a bright leading edge and a trailing ribbon that curves upward-right.
The shape is broader than any incoming projectile so successful reflection feels like a reward state.
Arc spacing leaves room for combo popup timing without obscuring the player success pose.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the duel loop, projectile attributes, UI layout, and Bevy runtime constraints for Reflect Academy.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the warm academy hall mood, layered duel composition, and luminous spell contrast.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the student duelist silhouette, costume trim, wand handling, and heroic stance.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load reflected_spell_arc.png during Loading and build a 2x2 TextureAtlasLayout for the outbound counter entity.
runtime_notes: Play at 14 FPS while the arc travels from the player lane toward the enemy hit region.
Use the authored upper-right curve as-is so the effect remains readable even during camera shake.
Despawn after the last frame or when collision with the enemy hit zone is resolved.

context.usage: Spawned immediately after a successful reflect and sent from the player lane toward the enemy podium.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 256x192 px for four 128x96 frames with no frame gutter.
The effect reads as a successful player-owned counter rather than an incoming enemy spell.
The leading edge stays bright and clearly oriented toward the enemy lane in every frame.
